title: Direct Local Execution Without Requiring Docker
description: The application shall support direct local execution using Node.js and React without requiring Docker. All components — frontend, backend, carrier simulator, and payroll simulator — shall be runnable natively on a developer's machine.
recommendedStartCommand: npm run dev:all (using concurrently or similar Node-based process manager to start frontend, backend, carrier simulator, and payroll simulator in one command)

title: Docker Containerization — Optional, Not Required
description: Docker containerization may be provided as an optional deployment mechanism but shall not be required for local development or demonstration. The application shall be fully functional without Docker.
rationale: Developer does not have Docker installed. Node.js + React direct execution is the primary path. Docker may be added later if remote deployment to cloud is required.

title: Documented Startup Process for All Local Services
description: The application shall provide a documented startup process that allows the frontend, backend, carrier simulator, and payroll simulator to be launched locally in a single command or via a simple documented procedure.
toolSuggestion: Use 'concurrently' Node.js package to run all services in one terminal with a single command.

title: Future Cloud Deployment Without Core Business Logic Changes
description: The application shall support a future cloud deployment without requiring changes to core business logic. Environment-specific differences shall be handled through configuration and deployment environment only.
preferredCloudTarget: Google Cloud Run (natural fit with Gemini API; also portable to AWS ECS/Fargate or Azure Container Apps)
cloudPortabilityPrinciple: Business logic shall not depend on any specific cloud provider.

title: Externalized Environment-Specific Configuration
description: Environment-specific configuration shall be externalized from application source code. The application shall support separate Local, Test, and Demo environment configurations.

title: Environment Variable Configuration for API Keys, Endpoints, and Runtime Settings
description: The application shall use environment variables for Gemini API configuration, service endpoints, ports, storage paths, and runtime settings.

title: Environment Files — .env.example Committed, Secrets Never Committed
description: API keys, credentials, and other secrets shall not be committed to source control. The .env.example file containing only variable names and safe defaults SHALL be committed. .env files containing actual secrets shall never be committed.

envFileConventions..env.example: Committed to Git — variable names and safe defaults only
envFileConventions..env.test: Automated test environment — NOT committed if it contains secrets
envFileConventions..env.demo: Demo environment — NOT committed if it contains secrets

title: Health and Readiness Endpoints
description: The application shall expose health and readiness endpoints that indicate the status of all dependencies.

healthEndpoints.GET /health: Liveness check — is the process alive?
healthEndpoints.GET /ready: Readiness check — are all dependencies up?

title: Startup Validation of Required Configuration and Dependencies
description: Application startup shall validate required configuration and dependencies. Do not silently start with broken configuration.
startupFailureBehavior: Log startup failure reason and exit with non-zero code. Example: 'Application startup FAILED: GEMINI_API_KEY is not configured.'

title: Demo Data Reset Mechanism
description: The demo shall provide a mechanism to restore seed data to a known baseline state, available only in development and demo environments.

title: Demo Reset Not Enabled in Production
description: Demo-only reset functionality (POST /api/demo/reset) shall not be enabled in production environments. The endpoint shall return HTTP 403 when NODE_ENV is not 'development' or 'demo'.

title: Cloud Container Deployment for Remote Customer Demonstrations
description: The application shall support deployment to a managed cloud container environment for remote demonstrations. The same application that runs locally shall be deployable to cloud without business logic changes.
cloudDataPersistence: Cloud deployments shall use persistent managed storage (database + object storage) rather than local file store.

title: Persistent Storage for Uploads and Generated Artifacts per Environment
description: Uploaded documents and generated artifacts shall use persistent storage appropriate to the deployment environment. Local: filesystem paths. Cloud: managed object storage.

title: Environment and Build Version Display
description: The application shall display environment name, application version, build number, and deployment date. A visible environment banner shall prevent confusion between local, test, and demo environments.
demoBanner: UI header banner showing: ACME GROUP BENEFITS DEMO | Environment: DEMO | Version: 1.4.0

title: CI/CD Automated Test Gates Before Demo Build Promotion
description: CI/CD shall execute automated tests before deploying a new demo build. A deployment shall not be promoted if critical tests fail.

title: Carrier and Payroll Mock Services Consistently Provisioned
description: The application shall consistently provision the carrier and payroll mock services required for the demo. These mock services shall start with the application using a single command (e.g., npm run dev:all with concurrently).
note: Docker is optional. Use a Node-based process manager (concurrently, npm-run-all) to launch all services from a single command.
